title: Evangelion 222 preview VS 333 outcome
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Evangelion 2.22 preview VS 3.33 outcome             Quote Post by Mike of the Desert » Wed May 01, 2013 6:52 am Hi guys, this will be my first post here and I'm extremely happy to join this community. I've grown up with Evangelion and I'll begin following this forums as much as I can. ;) ...But after a third-watch of You can (not) Redo, hoping to understand some things a little better, a weird huge doubt came to my mind. The "Preview" of 3.0 showed after 2.0 credits is COMPLETELY different from what we now have. Do you guys ever discussed about this? Is there an opinion, a reason, a debate about this? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lGy4RimdR9o

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 01, 2013 7:03 am The difference was noticed from the start, of course. My personal take as of right now is that what is shown in the preview actually happens during the timeskip. That would at least be a way to salvage it instead of just saying "Nah, never happened". Of course, there's also plenty in the preview of 2.0 that didn't happen at all (or happened with very significant changes), so I guess I have no basis for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Wed May 01, 2013 7:05 am I'm 99.9999% sure that this is discussed in some other thread. (I don't remember where it is) But anyways.....There are few theories about this: -Anno scrapped " the original" script during the production of the film, because he didn't like it. -These scenes will be shown in 4.0 as flashbacks.

[]:             Quote Post by Mike of the Desert » Wed May 01, 2013 8:05 am I'd love to see those as flashbacks in 4.0, and I was sure that this was a known issue, I'd be curious to find this particular thread you're remembering... This just came up to my mind after this third view and it really ended stuck in my head lately.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Wed May 01, 2013 8:17 am Yeah it has been discussed many times but not on a dedicated thread. There is a high chance that the 2.22 preview covers the events that happened in the 14 years gap but for some reason Anno decided not to feature them on Q.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Wed May 01, 2013 8:24 am Dima wrote: There is a high chance that the 2.22 preview covers the events that happened in the 14 years gap but for some reason Anno decided not to feature them on Q.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Wed May 01, 2013 8:57 am Giji Shinka wrote: I'm thinking that the plot points/settings we see in 3.0 preview would have been too predictable to guess in 4.0. (in 3.0 we know) Edit: The emotional impact and confusion we get in 3.33 wouldn't have been as high if we knew everything from the time between 2.0 and 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Kerath » Wed May 01, 2013 10:34 am I think the preview may have been intentionally misleading in order to strengthen the feeling of "whats going on" in the first half of Q.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed May 01, 2013 1:46 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 01, 2013 5:12 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: I do think though that Rebuild would have been better served by never having any of these 'next episode' things at all whatsoever at the ends; it's just one more thing for people to have trouble getting over.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed May 01, 2013 5:14 pm Well, they hook us into the next movie. They can't be all that bad.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed May 01, 2013 5:33 pm The previews are probably the one big thing I wish they would've removed from the Rebuild franchise. That & the mid-movie eyecatches. This isn't TV guys, these are theatrical features!! Anywho, the previews have predominantly been useless from the get go. Based on the animation itself I'd say they're hastily put together purely to act as fanservice. A teaser works wonders when you're doing a weekly television show because even if you're running drastically behind schedule you still have over 70% of that following episode DONE. You know what the next episode is going to be emotionally, thematically, & visually. That's not the case with theatrical films where you have the time to adapt the story, change the focus, mold the look & allow new influences to direct your path. Even when a filmmaker has a very distinct idea of where he's taking the story in following sequels - things change. New ideas come forth & the story evolves. The previews have always struck me as a holdover from what I think Rebuild originally started out as back in 2006 - back when these were 3 films (two 45 minute movies that play back to back in my mind is one movie, fact) that were supposed to be rushed out into theaters over the span of roughly one year - a quick easy way to scratch up some cash to build captial for Khara. Knock out a few "Retrace of Evangelion" movies that's 90% copied animation from the TV show & get out as soon as you can. Clearly the original intetions of what Rebuild was meant to be has changed over the course of 7+ years - as it should - & I've always viewed these teasers as pretty useles. They don't really signify what the films are supposed to be about except in the loosest terms & really have no bearing on what we should expect from the final film. The teaser for 4.0 looks particularly rushed. Based on what's left of Unit 02 there's no way a Unit 08 + 02 that looks like that is even practically feasible & I'd expect the MP Evas - if there are MP Evas - look like Mark 6 because they have the Mark 6 CG model from the third film sitting in a computer ready to use. Change the color to green & BOOM, a new Eva. * Our relationships to A LOT of franchise films - particularly sequels - would be drastically warped if they all had pointless teasers attached to them.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 01, 2013 5:50 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: * Our relationships to A LOT of franchise films - particularly sequels - would be drastically warped if they all had pointless teasers attached to them.
